skill.name: chembl-database
skill.description: Query ChEMBL bioactive molecules, targets, drugs, and assay data for medicinal chemistry research.
skill.summary: Search ChEMBL compounds, targets, bioactivities, drugs, and structure relationships.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium indicators, but review found they are mainly Markdown code fences, inline API field names, and official ChEMBL resource links. The skill has expected network dependency on the public ChEMBL service and an installation command, with no evidence of credential access, obfuscation, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code markers, and Python examples. The reviewed lines document ChEMBL client usage and installation steps rather than hidden command execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation or Python API examples. No shell execution wrapper, dynamic command construction, or automatic execution path was found.

low_findings.title: Expected Public Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to official ChEMBL, EBI, GitHub, GitBook, and notebook documentation resources. Network access is intrinsic to querying ChEMBL data and is not used for exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible public documentation links and match the stated ChEMBL database purpose. No suspicious destination or secret-bearing request was identified.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic API calls or hash use in executable code. The matched lines are descriptive text, bioactivity terminology, or field names in documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files contain documentation and query examples, not encryption or hashing logic. No weak algorithm usage such as MD5 or SHA1 operations was found.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts map to scientific text, query examples, and rate-limit guidance. They do not inspect the host, enumerate local systems, or probe networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations discuss cheminformatics, ChEMBL result fields, or responsible API use. No host discovery, port scanning, or environment enumeration behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Drug discovery teams need fast access to curated compound, target, and assay data. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code query ChEMBL for medicinal chemistry research.
actual_capabilities: Search ChEMBL molecules by identifier, name, structure, and molecular properties.
Retrieve target records for proteins, enzymes, organisms, and target classes.
Query bioactivity measurements such as IC50, Ki, Kd, and EC50 values.
Find similar compounds and substructures from SMILES strings.
Look up drug mechanisms, indications, and compound-target relationships.
Convert ChEMBL query results into tabular analysis workflows.
limitations: Requires the ChEMBL Python client and internet access to query live data.
Results depend on ChEMBL coverage, curation status, and service availability.
Does not validate compounds for clinical safety or regulatory use.
Large queries may need caching, filtering, or bulk download workflows.

use_cases.title: Find Potent Target Inhibitors
use_cases.description: Identify compounds with activity values below a chosen threshold for a selected biological target.

use_cases.title: Compare Compound Properties
use_cases.description: Filter molecules by molecular weight, LogP, hydrogen bonding, and related drug-likeness fields.

use_cases.title: Review Drug Mechanisms
use_cases.description: Retrieve known mechanisms, indications, and activity records for approved drugs or candidates.

prompt_templates.title: Look Up a Molecule
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ChEMBL database skill to look up CHEMBL25. Summarize the preferred name, formula, molecular weight, and available drug information.

prompt_templates.title: Search for Target Activities
prompt_templates.prompt: Find ChEMBL IC50 activities for EGFR with values at or below 100 nM. Return the main compound identifiers, values, units, and assay context.

prompt_templates.title: Filter Drug-Like Compounds
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ChEMBL for molecules with molecular weight under 500 and calculated LogP under 5. Explain how to review the results for duplicates and validity flags.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a SAR Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a ChEMBL workflow for a structure-activity relationship review using a SMILES query, similarity search, target bioactivities, and property comparison.

output_examples.input: Find potent EGFR inhibitors in ChEMBL.
output_examples.output: A ranked activity summary with ChEMBL compound identifiers, IC50 values, units, target identifier, and notes about data validity.
A concise compound profile with the ChEMBL identifier, preferred name, molecular properties, known mechanisms, and related activity records.

output_examples.input: Create a virtual screening workflow for kinase-like compounds.
output_examples.output: A stepwise workflow covering target search, property filters, activity thresholds, result export, and checks for duplicates or inconsistent units.

best_practices: Start with narrow target, activity type, and unit filters before expanding searches.
Check data validity comments, duplicate flags, and units before ranking compounds.
Use caching or bulk data options for repeated or large-scale ChEMBL analysis.
anti_patterns: Do not treat ChEMBL activity values as clinical proof of safety or efficacy.
Do not compare activities across assays without reviewing assay context and units.
Do not run broad unbounded queries repeatedly against the public API.

faq.question: What database does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses ChEMBL, a curated public database of bioactive molecules, targets, assays, and drug information.

faq.question: Does this skill require internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. Live ChEMBL queries require network access through the ChEMBL Python client.

faq.question: Can it search by chemical structure?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports similarity and substructure workflows using SMILES strings through ChEMBL endpoints.

faq.question: Can it export results for analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples show how to convert query results into pandas data frames for downstream analysis.

faq.question: Are the returned activities always comparable?
faq.answer: No. Review assay context, standard units, validity comments, and duplicate flags before comparing values.

faq.question: Is this a medical advice tool?
faq.answer: No. It supports research data retrieval and does not provide clinical, safety, or regulatory conclusions.
